headline: How The Copart (CPRT) Investment Story Is Shifting With New Price Targets And Risks
summary: Price targets on Copart have been reset lower, with JPMorgan moving from US$45 to US$34 and another firm trimming its target from US$33 to US$32, even as the model fair value holds at US$42.67. These changes reflect analyst concerns around fee rate competition, execution risk, and a recent fiscal Q2 miss, while still supporting active coverage and, in JPMorgan’s case, a Neutral stance. Read on to see how to interpret these shifting targets and what to watch as the Copart story develops from...

headline: The Closed End Fund Paying 6.7% That Rivals the S&P 500

headline: How The Hikma Pharmaceuticals (LSE:HIK) Narrative Is Shifting As Analyst Targets Reset
summary: The modelled fair value for Hikma Pharmaceuticals has been cut from £20.47 to £19.06 per share, with published price targets across the street now generally clustering in the £16.00 to £20.00 range. Some firms, such as JPMorgan with its £20.00 Overweight call, still see room for upside, while others have shifted to Underweight at £16.00, reflecting different readings of the same developments. Read on to see what is driving these diverging views and how you can track this evolving...
